patient_assessment.capability_summary: This 67-year-old male demonstrates good functional capacity with minimal symptoms (pain avg 0.44, symptoms avg 1.2) and average functional strength (STS benchmark 'Average'). He can tolerate weight-bearing positions (all scores ≤1) but has trunk sway and valgus knee alignment indicating dynamic instability. Quadruped position is safe (kneeling score 1).

selected_exercises.reasoning: Addresses posterior chain flexibility (cannot touch toes) with glute_max and hamstring activation. Safe supine position with beginner difficulty appropriate for patient's functional level. Provides foundation for hip extension pattern.

Progresses from double-leg bridging to address dynamic knee instability (valgus) through glute_med_min stabilization (value 4). Core_contra=true helps with anti-rotation control. Addresses posterior chain flexibility with glute_max:5 and hamstring activation.

Directly targets glute_med_min (primary mover value 4) to address valgus knee alignment and dynamic instability. Core_contra=true provides anti-rotation training. Side-lying position is safe and isolates hip abductors effectively.

Progresses from clamshell with higher glute_med_min activation (value 5) and core_ipsi=true to address trunk sway and core instability. Side-lying position remains safe while challenging hip stability and core control in functional alignment.

biomechanical_targets.strategy: Dynamic knee instability usually associates with weak core anti-rotation control, to prioritize exercises with `core_contra=true`. Prioritize exercises with high glute_med_min in muscles.primary_movers or muscles.secondary_movers (value 4-5). Match muscle role to functional capacity: for lower function patient, try to find those muscle target in muscles.primary_movers or muscles.secondary_movers; for higher function patient, prioritize finding those muscle target in muscles.stabiliser

biomechanical_targets.issue: Limited posterior chain flexibility (cannot touch toes)
biomechanical_targets.strategy: Prioritize exercises with high hamstring + glute_max in muscles (value 4-5 each)

weight_bearing_check.reasoning: STS benchmark performance is 'Average' (14 reps within 11-14 range). According to decision logic: 'Average' = MODERATE RISK → APPROVE WITH MODIFICATIONS. Patient has trunk sway present but hip sway absent. Overall weight-bearing capability is acceptable with modifications.

kneeling_check.reasoning: Kneeling score is 1 (Mild difficulty) and pain average is 0.44 (very low). According to decision logic: Kneeling score ≤ 2 = LOW RISK → APPROVE. Patient has minimal difficulty with kneeling and low pain levels.

core_stability_check.reasoning: Using flexible 'soft start' approach: Trunk sway present but hip sway absent. F2 standing score = 0 (no difficulty), SP4 twisting = 1 (mild difficulty). Function ADL normalized score = 125.5 (>70). According to decision logic: ONE sway present BUT function_ADL.normalized_0_100 > 70 = MODERATE RISK → APPROVE WITH MODIFICATIONS. Patient has good overall function despite trunk sway.

exercise_decisions.reasoning: Supine lying position only, no weight-bearing, kneeling, or core stability constraints triggered. Safe for all patients regardless of functional level. Addresses posterior chain flexibility (cannot touch toes) with glute_max and hamstring activation.

modifications: Start with double-leg bridging as warm-up
Monitor for trunk rotation or hip hiking
Progress slowly from 5 to 10 reps per side
Ensure neutral spine position throughout
exercise_decisions.reasoning: Core stability constraint triggered (core_contra=true). Patient has trunk sway present but good overall function (ADL normalized 125.5). Using soft start approach: MODERATE RISK → APPROVE WITH MODIFICATIONS. Exercise addresses valgus knee alignment through glute_med_min stabilization.

modifications: Ensure proper side-lying alignment with hips stacked
Start with small range of motion
Focus on controlled movement without trunk rotation
Progress to adding resistance band when form is stable
exercise_decisions.reasoning: Core stability constraint triggered (core_contra=true). Patient has trunk sway present but good overall function. Using soft start approach: MODERATE RISK → APPROVE WITH MODIFICATIONS. Directly targets glute_med_min to address valgus knee alignment. Side-lying position is inherently stable.

modifications: Start with short holds (10-15 seconds)
Use thick padding under knees for comfort
Focus on maintaining straight line from head to knees
Progress duration gradually as tolerance improves
Monitor for trunk sagging or hip dropping
exercise_decisions.reasoning: Core stability constraint triggered (core_ipsi=true). Patient has trunk sway present but good overall function. Using soft start approach: MODERATE RISK → APPROVE WITH MODIFICATIONS. This exercise progresses from clamshell with higher glute_med_min activation and addresses core control. Kneeling position is safe (kneeling score=1).

final_prescription.clinical_rationale: Foundational posterior chain exercise addressing hamstring tightness (cannot touch toes). Activates glute_max (4) and hamstrings (3) in safe supine position. Provides hip extension pattern foundation for more advanced exercises. Beginner difficulty appropriate for patient's functional level.

modifications: Start with double-leg bridging as warm-up
Monitor for trunk rotation or hip hiking
Progress slowly from 5 to 10 reps per side
Ensure neutral spine position throughout
final_prescription.clinical_rationale: Progresses from double-leg bridging to address dynamic knee instability (valgus) through glute_med_min stabilization (value 4). Core_contra=true provides anti-rotation control to address trunk sway. Higher glute_max activation (5) strengthens posterior chain. Modifications ensure safe progression given trunk sway present.

modifications: Ensure proper side-lying alignment with hips stacked
Start with small range of motion
Focus on controlled movement without trunk rotation
Progress to adding resistance band when form is stable
